Rationale: AI Validation1 likely excluded this paper under Code 3 (Firm/Team Level) due to its emphasis on 'dyadic analysis', 'degree-symmetric constructs', and firm-level outcomes (Logistics Innovation). However, a strict application of the AGENTS.md rules supports the Human Ground Truth (1 = Include). Per Rule 15 (Proxy Guardrail), we must check the survey referent. The boundary spanning construct 'Knowledge exchange' explicitly uses the individual referent 'I' (e.g., 'I exchange ideas with members of my host firm to find solutions to problems'), confirming it measures individual-level behavior rather than acting as a firm proxy. This behavior satisfies Rule 18 (Purposive Action) as it involves intentional problem-solving and task coordination, not mere communication frequency. Regarding Rule 13 (Dyadic Data Guardrail), the sample consists of N=81 independent, 1-to-1 matched dyads (81 unique LSP implants and 81 unique customers), which does not violate statistical independence. Finally, while the structural model tests dyadic relationships, Table 5 ('logistics service provider responses') provides the unaggregated, zero-order correlations for the individual boundary spanners. Per Rule 14, since the paper reports individual employee boundary spanning behavior and valid statistical effect sizes, it must be included.
